---
layout: paper
title: "Neural Access Method™ v1.0 — Master Bibliography"
doi: "10.5281/zenodo.XXXXXXX"
---

# Neural Access Method™ v1.0 — Master Bibliography

**Back to:** [Main Paper](index.html) | [Visualizations](nam-visualizations.html) | [Cross-References](nam-cross-references.html)

*References contextualize observed mechanisms in neuroscience, motor learning, and performance science. The NAM protocol derives from practitioner observation across multiple domains before formal naming.*

---

## Motor Learning and Procedural Memory

Beilock, S. L., & Carr, T. H. (2001). On the fragility of skilled performance: What governs choking under pressure? *Journal of Experimental Psychology: General*, 130(4), 701–725. https://doi.org/10.1037/0096-3445.130.4.701

Ericsson, K. A., Krampe, R. T., & Tesch-Römer, C. (1993). The role of deliberate practice in the acquisition of expert performance. *Psychological Review*, 100(3), 363–406. https://doi.org/10.1037/0033-295X.100.3.363

Fitts, P. M., & Posner, M. I. (1967). *Human performance*. Brooks/Cole.

McKenzie, I. A., et al. (2014). Motor skill learning requires active central myelination. *Science*, 346(6207), 318–322. https://doi.org/10.1126/science.1254960

Schmidt, R. A. (1975). A schema theory of discrete motor skill learning. *Psychological Review*, 82(4), 225–260. https://doi.org/10.1037/h0076770

Wulf, G., McNevin, N., & Shea, C. H. (2001). The automaticity of complex motor skill learning as a function of attentional focus. *Quarterly Journal of Experimental Psychology*, 54A(4), 1143–1154. https://doi.org/10.1080/713756012

Xiao, L., et al. (2016). Rapid production of new oligodendrocytes is required in the earliest stages of motor-skill learning. *Nature Neuroscience*, 19(9), 1210–1217. https://doi.org/10.1038/nn.4351

---

## Cognitive Load and Pressure Performance

Arnsten, A. F. T. (2009). Stress signalling pathways that impair prefrontal cortex structure and function. *Nature Reviews Neuroscience*, 10(6), 410–422. https://doi.org/10.1038/nrn2648

Eysenck, M. W., Derakshan, N., Santos, R., & Calvo, M. G. (2007). Anxiety and cognitive performance: Attentional control theory. *Emotion*, 7(2), 336–353. https://doi.org/10.1037/1528-3542.7.2.336

LeDoux, J. E. (1996). *The emotional brain*. Simon & Schuster.

Nørretranders, T. (1998). *The user illusion: Cutting consciousness down to size*. Viking.

---

## Identity and Narrative

McAdams, D. P. (1993). *The stories we live by: Personal myths and the making of the self*. William Morrow.

Markus, H. (1977). Self-schemata and processing information about the self. *Journal of Personality and Social Psychology*, 35(2), 63–78. https://doi.org/10.1037/0022-3514.35.2.63

---

## Related Published Papers

Slusher, A. M. (2026a). *Neuroformation™ v1.0: A methodology for building resilience in adaptive systems*. Independent Researcher. https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.19197818

Slusher, A. M. (2026b). *The Elevation Grid™ v1.1: A neurobiological performance diagnostic framework*. Independent Researcher. https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.18790842

Slusher, A. M. (2026c). *Neural Access Method™ v1.0* (this paper). Independent Researcher. https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.XXXXXXX

---

*2026 © Aaron M. Slusher · Independent Researcher · [Main Paper](index.html)*
